title: What I think about Colorado - Request

transcript.text: Hello, here's a request video. I can't even remember who it's from, but uh, yeah, uh, what do I think about Colorado? Um, because that's where I'm at right now. Yeah, yeah, Colorado is my home. Um, I moved here when I, I moved here when I was six, um, from, from, uh, Austin, Texas, uh, to Boulder and, uh, and then went to college in Durango and then, uh, and then I

Lived with, you know, with my mom intermittently since then. So Boulder. So, uh, yeah, Colorado, I think it's my favorite state in America. So there's people who live in Colorado and, but it's like, but it might as well be Kansas.

A lot of Europeans who've been there say they don't like it though, because it's on a, it's on a grid system more, more or less, except for the downtown, which is more diagonally oriented, but which, which makes it too easy to get around, makes it boring for Europeans who like the confusing, narrow, windy streets.

But, uh, there's a good view of the big mountains next to Denver. Right next to Denver, there's this, there's Mount Evan, which is a 14,000 foot mountain that you can drive a car all the way to the top of and see all the other mountains in Colorado.
